cases.description: Verify Sign Up with Email screen UI, validations and navigation
steps:
1. Open the app.1. Open the app.
2. Complete onboarding and tap Get Started.
3. Verify ON_02B_Sign Up Options (Android) screen is displayed.
2. Navigate to ON_02A_Sign Up Options (iOS) and tap Sign up with Email.
3. Verify ON_02C_Sign Up with Email screen is displayed.
4. Verify Back to Sign Up Options link is visible.
5. Verify Full Name, Email, and Password fields are visible.
6. Verify Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy links are displayed.
7. Verify Sign Up button is disabled when fields are empty.
8. Enter valid Full Name, Email, and Password.
9. Verify Sign Up button becomes enabled.
11. Enter an invalid email and verify error message.
12. Enter an invalid password and verify error message.
14. Tap Back to Sign Up Options.
cases.expected: User is redirected to ON_02C_Sign Up with Email. All UI elements are displayed correctly. Sign Up button remains disabled until all mandatory fields are entered. After entering valid data, the button becomes enabled and user can proceed. Invalid email shows ON_09A_Sign Up - Invalid Email. Invalid password shows ON_05C_Sign Up - Invalid Password. Tapping Privacy Policy opens ON_02G_Privacy Policy. Tapping Back to Sign Up Options returns to ON_02A_Sign Up Options (iOS) or ON_02B_Sign Up Options (Android).

cases.description: Verify all input validation and system error states for Sign Up and Log In
steps: 1. Navigate to "Sign Up with Email"; trigger ON_05A, ON_05B, and ON_05C by entering invalid data/existing email/weak password. 2. Attempt "Sign Up" with valid data but simulate server failure to trigger ON_05D. 3. Tap "Resend Email" and simulate failure for ON_05E. 4. Navigate to "Log In"; enter wrong credentials (ON_05F) and simulate system failure (ON_05G).

cases.description: Verify the complete application flow from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen using the Sign Up Later option.
steps: 1. Install the application on the device.
3. Verify that the Splash Screen displays the HP logo with the "sprocket" text in the center of the screen.
4. Wait until the Splash Screen is completed automatically.
5. Verify that the first onboarding screen is displayed with the Sprocket logo, onboarding image, page indicator, title, description, and "Swipe to continue." text.
6. Swipe from right to left to navigate to the second onboarding screen.
7. Verify that the second onboarding screen displays the Sprocket logo, AI image, page indicator, "AI Editing & Generation" title, description, and "Swipe to continue." text.
8. Swipe from right to left to navigate to the third onboarding screen.
9. Verify that the third onboarding screen displays the Sprocket logo, image stack, page indicator, "Inkless & Seamless" title, description, compatibility note, and "Swipe to continue." text.
10. Swipe from right to left to navigate to the fourth onboarding screen.
11. Verify that the fourth onboarding screen displays the Sprocket logo, onboarding image, page indicator, "Unleash Your Creativity" title, description, and the green "Get Started!" button.
13. Verify that the Sign Up screen is displayed.
14. Verify that the "I already have an account" button is displayed.
15. Verify that the "Sign Up Later" button is displayed.
17. Verify that the green loading indicator is displayed for a few seconds.
18. Verify that the Home screen is displayed successfully.
cases.expected: The application successfully navigates from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen.

verifications.description: Verify the complete application flow from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen using the Sign Up Later option.
verifications.expected: The application successfully navigates from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen.

verifications.description: Verify the complete application flow from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen using the Sign Up Later option.
verifications.expected: The application successfully navigates from the Splash Screen to the Home Screen.
verifications.verification: Verify navigation marker: Sign Up Later

cases.module: Facebook Gallery - No Internet Tag Search
cases.description: Verify the No Internet screen is displayed when the user tries to search Facebook photos using tags without an internet connection.
steps:
1. Install the application on the device.
2. Launch the application.
3. Verify that the Splash Screen displays the HP logo with the "sprocket" text.
4. Wait until the Splash Screen is completed automatically.
5. Swipe from right to left to complete all four onboarding screens.
6. Verify that the fourth onboarding screen displays the "Get Started!" button.
7. Tap the "Get Started!" button.
8. Verify that the Sign Up screen is displayed.
9. Tap the "Sign Up Later" button.
10. Wait until the Home screen is displayed successfully.
11. If the notification permission popup is displayed, tap the "Allow" button.
12. Tap the "Quick Print" option.
13. If the Gallery permission popup is displayed, tap the "Allow all" button.
14. Verify that the Gallery screen is displayed successfully.
15. Verify that the Back arrow is displayed at the top left side, the folder name "Recent" is displayed at the top center, and the Sort icon is displayed at the top right side.
16. Tap the folder name "Recent".
17. Tap the "Facebook" option.
18. Wait until the Facebook Gallery is displayed successfully.
19. Turn off the internet connection on the device.
20. Tap the Tag icon at the bottom right side of the Facebook Gallery screen.
21. Verify that the center of the screen displays the bold text "No Internet".
22. Verify that the description "Please connect to the Internet and try again." is displayed below the title.
23. Verify that the tag search text field is displayed in the disabled state.
24. Verify that the previously entered tag, if any, is displayed but cannot be edited while there is no internet connection.
25. Verify that the Back arrow is displayed at the top left side.
26. Verify that the Facebook folder name is displayed at the top center.
27. Verify that the Sort icon is displayed at the top right side.
28. Verify that the bottom navigation bar displays "More", "Create", and "Printer", and that the "Create" tab is highlighted by default.
cases.expected: The application displays the "No Internet" screen with the correct message, disables the tag search field, and prevents tag searching until the internet connection is restored.

verifications.module: Facebook Gallery - No Internet Tag Search
verifications.description: Verify the No Internet screen is displayed when the user tries to search Facebook photos using tags without an internet connection.
verifications.expected: The application displays the "No Internet" screen with the correct message, disables the tag search field, and prevents tag searching until the internet connection is restored.

13. If the Gallery permission popup is displayed, tap the "Allow all" button.
14. Verify that the Gallery screen is displayed successfully.
15. Verify that the Back arrow is displayed at the top left side of the screen.
16. Verify that the folder name is displayed at the top center.
17. Verify that the Sort icon is displayed at the top right side of the screen.
18. Tap the folder name displayed at the top center.
19. Select a folder that does not contain any photos.
20. Verify that the Gallery screen is refreshed automatically.
21. Verify that the center of the screen displays the "No Photos Found" illustration.
22. Verify that the text "No photos found." is displayed below the illustration.
23. Verify that no photo thumbnails are displayed on the screen.
24. Verify that the Back arrow remains visible at the top left side.
25. Verify that the selected folder name remains displayed at the top center.
26. Verify that the Sort icon remains displayed at the top right side.
27. Verify that the screen remains responsive and does not crash or freeze.
28. Select another folder that contains photos.
29. Verify that the "No photos found." message disappears automatically.
30. Verify that the photo thumbnails are displayed successfully for the selected folder.
cases.expected: When a folder contains no photos, the application displays the No Photos Found illustration and message. When the user selects another folder that contains photos, the message disappears and the photo thumbnails are displayed correctly.

steps: 1. Launch app, complete onboarding (ON_01B–ON_01E), and tap "Get Started!". 2. Connect HP Sprocket Studio Plus. 3. Select image(s), open Print Preview, and tap Print. 4. The interrupted print job remains at the top of the queue, and all pending print jobs are retained and are not discarded.

cases.description: Verify session cancellation while photo is processing
steps:
1. Launch the app and wait for the Splash screen. 
2. Complete onboarding (ON_01B to ON_01E). 
3. Tap Get Started! and on the Sign Up screen tap "Sign Up Later". 
4. Verify the Home screen appears. 
5. Tap the Photobooth module.
 6. Verify onboarding popup appears for the first time. 
7. Verify popup text and buttons are displayed correctly. 
8. Allow camera permission by tapping "While using the app". 9. Verify the Photobooth Camera screen opens successfully. 10. Verify top options: Back button and title are visible. 
11. Verify bottom options: Flash, Shutter, and Switch Camera icons are visible. 
12. Verify Flash is OFF and Front Camera is selected by default. 13. Tap the Shutter button. 
14. During countdown or while a photo is being processed, tap the Square Stop button. 
15. Observe the screen behavior.
cases.expected: Photobooth session stops immediately and captured photos are discarded successfully.

verifications.description: Verify session cancellation while photo is processing
verifications.expected: Photobooth session stops immediately and captured photos are discarded successfully.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify No Paper Tray Error during firmware update
steps: 1. Launch the application and wait for splash screen to complete. 2. Complete onboarding by swiping until Get Started appears. 3. Tap Get Started → Sign Up Later. 4. User lands on Home screen. 5. Tap Printer icon. 6. User is redirected to No Printer Added screen. 7. Tap Added a New Printer. 8. Select HP Sprocket Studio Plus printer. 9. Proceed through Wi-Fi setup flow and complete printer connection. 10. Navigate to Printer Settings screen. 11. Remove paper tray from printer (simulate no tray condition). 12. Tap Update Available button. 13. System attempts firmware update validation. 14. Verify DE_08L error screen is displayed.
cases.expected: Error message is displayed: A "No Paper Tray" error popup is displayed with the message "Please attach the paper tray (loaded with paper) to complete the firmware update." The popup includes a "Try Again" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The firmware update cannot continue until the paper tray is attached.

cases.description: Verify Try Again functionality after resolving hardware issue
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding flow. 2. Navigate to Printer Settings screen. 3. Trigger any firmware error condition (paper/jam/ribbon). 4. Verify corresponding error screen is displayed. 5. Fix the hardware issue (insert paper / clear jam / insert ribbon). 6. Tap Try Again button on error screen. 7. System re-validates printer status. 8. Verify firmware process resumes and navigates to HPS_FW_04 While Installing screen if issue is resolved.
cases.expected: System retries firmware update successfully after issue is resolved and continues installation flow.

verifications.description: Verify Try Again functionality after resolving hardware issue

The popup displays the bold title "Very Low Battery", followed by the message "Your printer battery is too low. Charge your printer and try printing again." The popup includes an "OK" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The print job is stopped until the printer is sufficiently charged.

cases.description: Verify user is redirected to Custom SDK screen after tapping Edit icon.
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding flow.
5. Tap Printer and connect printer successfully.
6. Select an image from Gallery or Facebook.
7. Tap the selected image and verify Print Preview screen opens.
8. Verify Share icon at top-right and Back arrow at top-left.
9. Verify Copies and Tiles options are displayed and Copies is selected by default.
10. Verify AI icon at bottom-left, Print button at center and Edit icon at bottom-right.

cases.description: Verify that the printer carousel is not an infinite loop and has defined start and end points.
steps: 2.  Wait for splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until final screen
11. Swipe right until Studio Plus (first printer) is displayed.
12. Attempt to swipe further right multiple times.
13. Verify that no additional printers are displayed.
14. Swipe left until Sprocket Panorama (last printer) is displayed.
15. Attempt to swipe further left multiple times.
16. Verify that no additional printers are displayed.
cases.expected: The printer carousel should have a definitive start (Sprocket Studio Plus) and end (Sprocket Panorama). Swiping beyond either boundary should not wrap around to the opposite end of the list. The carousel should not behave as an infinite loop.

verifications.description: Verify that the printer carousel is not an infinite loop and has defined start and end points.
verifications.expected: The printer carousel should have a definitive start (Sprocket Studio Plus) and end (Sprocket Panorama). Swiping beyond either boundary should not wrap around to the opposite end of the list. The carousel should not behave as an infinite loop.

cases.description: Verify 'Charge + Power On' Screen UI for the HP Sprocket 200
steps: 2. Wait for the splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until the final screen.
11. Wait to load 'Select Your Device' screen
12. Scroll the printer carousel until 200 is displayed.

The opened screen should display the following:

1. The 'Charge + Power On' screen should be displayed.
2. The printer image should be displayed.
3. The following instructional text should be displayed:

4. The 'Next' button should be displayed and enabled.
5. The 'Close (X)' button should be displayed and enabled.
6. Step 1 of the setup progress indicator should be highlighted.

cases.description: Verify Printer Found After Retry for HP 200 printer
steps: Make sure mobile native bluetooth is enabled.
2. Wait for the splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until the final screen.
11. Scroll the printer carousel until 200 is displayed.
13. Tap Next on the Charge + Power On screen.
14. Tap Next on the Load Paper screen.
16. Ensure no supported printer is powered on or available nearby.
17. Wait for the search timeout period to complete.
18. Navigate to the No Printers Found screen.
cases.expected:
1. The Searching for Printers screen should be displayed.
2. The available printer should be discovered.
3. The printer should appear in the printer list.

verifications.description: Verify Printer Found After Retry for HP 200 printer
verifications.expected:
1. The Searching for Printers screen should be displayed.
2. The available printer should be discovered.
3. The printer should appear in the printer list.

verifications.description: Verify Printer Found After Retry for HP 200 printer
verifications.expected:
1. The Searching for Printers screen should be displayed.
2. The available printer should be discovered.
3. The printer should appear in the printer list.

cases.description: Verify No Printer Found Again After Retry
steps: Make sure mobile native bluetooth is enabled.
2. Wait for the splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until the final screen.
11. Scroll the printer carousel until 200 is displayed.
13. Tap Next on the Charge + Power On screen.

verifications.description: Verify No Printer Found Again After Retry

cases.description: Verify 'Charge + Power On' Screen UI for the HP Sprocket 3x4
steps: 2. Wait for the splash screen to complete.
3. Swipe onboarding screens until the final screen.
11. Wait to load 'Select Your Device' screen
12. Scroll the printer carousel until Sprocket 3x4 is displayed.
cases.expected:
The opened screen should display the following:

1. The 'Charge + Power On' screen should be displayed.
2. The printer image should be displayed.
3. The following instructional text should be displayed:
"Plug in the USB cord to charge and automatically power on the printer."
"When not charging, press and hold the power button until the LEDs light up."
4. The 'Next' button should be displayed and enabled.
5. The 'Close (X)' button should be displayed and enabled.
6. Step 1 of the setup progress indicator should be highlighted.

cases.module: This Printer uses wi-fi pop up
cases.description: Verify 'This Printer uses wi-fi screen' for Android
steps: 1. Make sure the HP Sprocket Studio Plus printer is powered on and phone is not connected with any network.
3. Wait for the splash screen to complete.
4. Swipe through the onboarding screens until the final screen.
8. Tap 'Printer' from the bottom navigation bar.
12. Scroll the printer carousel until 'HP Sprocket Studio Plus' is displayed.

15. Complete the HP600 setup onboarding until the 'Ready for Wi‑Fi Connection' screen is displayed.
17. Wait to load the Available HP600 Printers at  'Searching for Printers' screen
19. Wait to load the 'This printer uses wi-fi' screen
cases.expected:
Verify that the following UI elements are displayed correctly:

1. Close (X) button is displayed and enabled.
2. Wi-Fi illustration/icon is displayed.
3. Title "This printer uses Wi-Fi" is displayed.
4. Description text "Your phone is not connected to a Wi-Fi network." is displayed.
5. Instruction card with the heading "To use Sprocket Studio Plus:" is displayed.
6. Step 1: "Open your phone's Wi-Fi settings." is displayed.
7. Step 2: "Connect to a 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network. (5 GHz networks are not compatible)." is displayed.
8. Step 3: "Return to the HP Sprocket App." is displayed.
9. Cancel button is displayed and enabled.
10. Open Settings button is displayed and enabled.
11. "No Wi-Fi Available? Tap Here" link is displayed and enabled.
12. All UI elements are properly aligned with no truncation or overlapping text.
